title: would you buy a Evangelion Comic drawn by KENNETH ROCAFORT
display_title: would you buy a Evangelion Comic drawn by KENNETH ROCAFORT
wikitext:
[]: would you buy a Evangelion Comic drawn by KENNETH ROCAFORT?             Quote Post by movieartman » Mon Feb 24, 2014 8:30 pm Would you be interested in buying a Evangelion comic drawn by artist Kenneth rocafort? he is currently on the main Superman comic for dc comics. here is a EVA sketch by him. his mechanics and landscapes are breathtaking, and i think would be a brilliant fit for evangelion examples of his work http://static.comicvine.com/uploads/original/0/40/805159-_2.jpg http://1.bp.blogspot.com/_vj2e1m7Hlgw/TJQHM8v_4GI/AAAAAAAArUo/O_GVYwXSvq4/s1600/velo.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Feb 24, 2014 8:41 pm I'm a little confused about this thread's purpose. Is there some behind the scenes deal for a western-style Eva comic book* going on? Or is this just a general question? Lovely drawing of Eva-01, at any rate. *IMO, adapting something from anime style to western style or vice versa doesn't automatically mean it'll look bad. I think it all depends on the artist's individual style.

[]:             Quote Post by movieartman » Mon Feb 24, 2014 8:52 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Is there some behind the scenes deal for a western-style Eva comic book* going on? Or is this just a general question?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Feb 24, 2014 9:07 pm While his art is quite beautiful I whole-heartedly disagree that it's in any way suitable for Eva. Anno and co. spent the entirety of Rebuild refining the look of Eva to perfection, without care for budgets or schefules, and that's the Eva I want more of.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Mon Feb 24, 2014 10:52 pm I personally love Rocafort's art but have pretty much zero interest in seeing a comicbook adaptation of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Feb 25, 2014 12:50 am Reminds me a lot of Sam Keith, specifically in just how dense the linework is, but more realistic where Keith tends towards absurd exaggerations.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Feb 25, 2014 12:20 pm I know this is his take, but he brought up the "beard" too much. Still, it would be worth checking out other interpretations of Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by LegionWrex » Wed Feb 26, 2014 8:41 pm I would be interested... if I had money Cause I don't have any...

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Tue Mar 25, 2014 5:01 am Aside from the Progressive Goatee, I like that Unit 01 head a lot. I'd be interested in seeing some more re-imaginings, that's for certain. His style is extremely cool. A whole comic...depends how well he really understands the story I suppose.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Tue Mar 25, 2014 7:47 am The art style is interesting. Far from my favorite and i don't know if it would fit Eva too well, but it would be interesting to see his visual interpretation of Evangelion. I think i would prefer an art/concepts book over a comic book though.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Mar 25, 2014 4:44 pm If he's adapting NGE or ENT, sure. If it's something original, pass. Unless it's backstory, a la the story of the KE or something. Then maybe.
